_comment: ubuntnix archive lockfile (SPEC.md §4.4; GitHub issue #7, milestone M1). Two-tier per the reproducibility model: `public` pins a snapshot.ubuntu.com timestamp plus resolved (name, version, arch, component, path, sha256, size) tuples for the archive.ubuntu.com/security.ubuntu.com pockets the snapshot service covers; `esm` uses the identical per-package schema but is intentionally an empty list until milestone M4 wires up esm.ubuntu.com fetching against a Pro token (no snapshot service exists for esm -- SPEC.md §4.4, §8.2, R4). Consumed by nix/archive.nix via builtins.fromJSON; see that file for fetch mechanics and tests/unit/040-archive-lockfile.sh for the schema this file is held to. Every sha256 below was independently verified by downloading the .deb from snapshot.ubuntu.com and hashing it locally -- the resolved index value was not trusted blindly (see the Engineer's report for issue #7).

packages._comment: Added for issue #9 (rootfs composition): a minimal-dependency (Depends: debconf only, already present in ubuntu-base) package whose postinst provably consumes a debconf answer -- tzdata/Areas + tzdata/Zones/<Area> select the /etc/timezone content and the /etc/localtime symlink target. Used by nix/compose.nix's compose-preseed-proof to demonstrate SPEC.md §6's ubuntnix.debconf preseed primitive actually reaches a maintainer script.
packages.name: tzdata

packages._comment: Added for issue #9 (rootfs composition, image-artifact step): provides /usr/bin/mksquashfs, the archive-sourced tool nix/compose.nix uses to produce the read-only squashfs rootfs image (all-Canonical rule -- SPEC.md §1.3 -- forbids sourcing this from nixpkgs). Needs liblzo2-2 below (its only runtime dependency not already present in ubuntu-base; libc6/liblz4-1/liblzma5/libzstd1/zlib1g are already part of ubuntu-base's own base package set).
packages.name: squashfs-tools

packages._comment: squashfs-tools' only runtime dependency not already shipped inside ubuntu-base -- see the squashfs-tools entry above.
packages.name: liblzo2-2

esm._comment: Empty until M4 (SPEC.md §4.4, R4): esm.ubuntu.com has no snapshot service, so this tier is pinned directly by (name, version, arch, component, path, sha256, size) once M4 wires up fetching against a Pro token. esm content is subscription-gated and never redistributed publicly.
